problem: when applying brakes nose pulled right. when taxing neutral rudders nose would pull left.
action: taxied a/c confirmed both discreps, with rudder pedals neutral a/c would pull Left. adjusted rudder trim to neutral and a/c taxied straight. Also found brakes to be very difficult to activate and then once activated brakes would remain activated for 10 seconds after releasing. inspected LH and RH calipers and pads, no defects found. cleaned caliper guide pins and pin holes. found brake reservoir fluid very dirty and thick, flushed entire brake fluid system and bled brakes. taxied a/c ops check good.

problem: When we checked brakes, the airplane would turn right. Had both brakes pressed but then the right rudder pedal would sink in as if it was loosing pressure. Tested brakes a few times but it kept turning right
action: Ground ran AC, confirmed pulling to the right with even braking pressure on both LH and RH brakes. Visually inspected brake system, no leaks detected. Removed and replaced both LH and RH brake linings with new, cleaned brake caliper pins, bleed LH brake, and serviced reservoir with MIL-PRF-5606. Ground ran AC, no further issues noted
